description:
A colleague has asked you to create a **repeatable, timestamped backup procedure** for a small web-project that lives in your home directory.  
  The project is laid out like this (all paths are already present when the task starts):

    /home/user/projects/portfolio_site/
        ├── assets/
        │     ├── logo.png
        │     └── hero.jpg
        ├── scripts/
        │     └── app.js
        └── styles/
              └── style.css

  Requirements

  1. Create a compressed archive containing ONLY the **assets** and **scripts** sub-directories (the *styles* directory must be excluded).  
     • Name of the archive:  
       /home/user/backups/portfolio_site_backup_20240101_120000.tar.gz  
     • The final archive **must preserve the two captured directories’ internal structure**.

  2. Generate a human-readable **backup log file** that documents the operation.  
     • Log file path:  
       /home/user/backup_logs/portfolio_site_backup_20240101_120000.log  
     • Required log file format (exactly, including the colon separators, all keys upper-case, one SPACE after every colon):  

       ARCHIVE_NAME: portfolio_site_backup_20240101_120000.tar.gz  
       ARCHIVE_SIZE: <size-in-bytes>  
       MD5: <32-char-lowercase-hex-digest>  
       FILES:  
       <one relative path per line, in ASCIIbetical order, of every file that ended up inside the archive>

     Example of the FILES section layout (your list will be different but this shows the required shape):

       FILES:
       assets/background.png
       assets/logo.svg
       scripts/app.js

     Notes about the log:
       • ARCHIVE_SIZE must contain the **unformatted byte count** reported by `stat --printf="%s" …` or equivalent.  
       • MD5 must be the checksum *after* the archive is successfully written.  
       • FILES list must be alphabetically sorted relative paths (no leading “./”).

  3. Place both the archive and the log file in the exact directories shown above.  
     • If the directories /home/user/backups or /home/user/backup_logs do not exist, create them with default user permissions.  
     • Do **not** move, rename, or delete the original source files.

  4. Use *at least one* backgrounded (“&”) or subshell pipeline command so that archiving and log generation can run in **parallel**. (The test suite won’t check *how* you achieved parallelism, only that you didn’t do everything sequentially in a single command.)

  Verification hints

  • The automated grader will extract the archive, check the files present, read the log, recompute the MD5, compare the recorded byte-size, and verify the relative paths list.  
  • Make sure the log exactly matches the format block above, including the final newline.  
  • Do not hard-code sizes or checksums—calculate them programmatically.  
  • Absolutely nothing else should be written to stdout except what the underlying Linux commands naturally emit.
truth:
Initial filesystem before the agent begins:

    /home/user/projects/portfolio_site/assets/logo.png    (11 bytes, content: "PNGLOGO123\n")
    /home/user/projects/portfolio_site/assets/hero.jpg    (11 bytes, content: "JPGHERO123\n")
    /home/user/projects/portfolio_site/scripts/app.js      (19 bytes, content: "console.log('hi');\n")
    /home/user/projects/portfolio_site/styles/style.css    (20 bytes, content: "body{background:#fff}\n")

  Directories /home/user/backups and /home/user/backup_logs do NOT exist initially.

  After correct task completion:

    1. /home/user/backups/portfolio_site_backup_20240101_120000.tar.gz exists.
       • When extracted it must produce:
           assets/logo.png
           assets/hero.jpg
           scripts/app.js
       • Assets retain the 11-byte original content; app.js retains its 19-byte content.
       • The styles/ directory and style.css must NOT be present.

    2. /home/user/backup_logs/portfolio_site_backup_20240101_120000.log exists.
       • It contains exactly five logical sections in the order specified in <task>.
       • ARCHIVE_SIZE value equals the real size (in bytes) of the .tar.gz file as reported by stat(1).
       • MD5 value equals md5sum of the generated .tar.gz file.
       • FILES list contains exactly three lines in ASCIIbetical order:
           assets/hero.jpg
           assets/logo.png
           scripts/app.js

    3. Both directories /home/user/backups and /home/user/backup_logs are created with default 755 and owned by user.

  The test harness will:
    • Verify existence and permissions of the directories.
    • Check that unwanted files (e.g., styles/style.css) are absent from the archive.
    • Parse the log file and confirm all keys and values match expectations.
name: task_000000_ef2a3eaa
